normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 4.5 - Carawatha Design Guidelines (Carawatha Primary School Redevelopment, North Lake Road, Willagee)
scope_summary: Design guidelines adopted as a local planning policy governing the residential redevelopment of the former Carawatha Primary School site at North Lake Road, Willagee (City of Melville). It sets a developer-endorsement process plus general and precinct-specific design provisions (Precinct A street lots, Precinct B mews/terrace lots, Precinct C apartment lots) covering setbacks, height, materials, roofs, fencing, parking, landscape and the public realm. Where inconsistent with the Structure Plan, other LPPs or the R-Codes, these Design Guidelines prevail for the estate.

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum building height to primary frontage
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Precinct A street lots and Precinct B mews lots
Precinct A dwellings (garage also min 1m behind two-storey element)
Precinct A - portions of upper storey not within permitted parapet wall extent

key_numeric_standards.topic: single storey boundary wall maximum height
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Precinct A side boundaries between front and rear setback lines

key_numeric_standards.topic: driveway maximum width across street reserve

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Precinct B pitched roofs visible from primary frontage (min eaves width 450mm)
Precinct C apartment lots (any fourth floor set back from floor below)
Precinct A lots - solid base max 0.9m with min 50% visually permeable panels above
primary frontage setback area landscaping, all lots

definitions: porch or bay window (minimum overall width 1.8m and projection of at least 0.6m)

cross_references.cited: Architectural and Urban Design Advisory Panel (Policy No. LPP1.2)
Provision of Public Art in Development Proposals (Policy No. LPP1.4)

quality_flags: OCR garbling on graphics-heavy pages: design-board captions and watermark text interleaved with body text (e.g. 'CARAWATHA SCPRHOJECTOOL' style artifacts), though substantive provisions remain legible
figures (development concept plan, precinct plans, materials palette) not represented in extracted text
version marker 'V6.5 08 February 2019' is the consultant document version; no council adoption resolution date appears in the extracted text
